purpose: Machine-readable list of FAR silent-assumption defaults for AI evaluator and authoring use.

fields.id: Stable identifier (A# = user-flagged top-priority, B.x.y = section-derived).
fields.rule: Default treatment when stem is silent.
fields.context: Stem signal/absence that triggers the rule.
fields.logic: GAAP/IFRS basis and how the trap is typically built.

rules.rule: Preferred stock is noncumulative when 'cumulative' is not stated.
rules.context: Stem describes preferred stock (e.g., '6% preferred') with no 'cumulative' qualifier anywhere.
rules.logic: Default classification is noncumulative; dividends in arrears do not carry forward. Drives EPS numerator (skip subtracting undeclared preferred dividends), dividend allocation, and RE appropriation.

rules.rule: Treasury stock uses the cost method when 'par value method' is not stated.
rules.context: Treasury stock reacquisition or reissuance is described with no method named.
rules.logic: Cost method: T/S recorded at acquisition cost as contra-equity. On reissue, differences hit APIC–Treasury first, then RE; never gain/loss to NI.

rules.rule: Lessee uses the rate implicit in the lease if readily determinable; IBR only when implicit is not determinable.
rules.context: Lessee lease problem with both implicit rate and IBR provided.
rules.logic: ASC 842 hierarchy: implicit → IBR → risk-free (private only). IBR is a fallback, not a choice. Lessors always use implicit.

rules.rule: Preferred stock is nonparticipating when 'participating' is not stated.
rules.logic: Preferred takes its stated rate; common takes residual. Drives two-class dividend allocations.

rules.rule: Preferred stock is nonconvertible when 'convertible' is not stated.
rules.logic: No diluted-EPS impact unless explicitly convertible.

rules.rule: Small stock dividend (<20–25%) recorded at fair value; large (≥25%) at par.
rules.context: 'Stock dividend' with no valuation method specified — size-based default applies.
rules.logic: Small dividends treated as quasi-cash (FV); large dividends treated like splits (par). Drives RE reduction amount.

rules.rule: Stock split: no journal entry, memo only, par reduced proportionally.
rules.context: '2-for-1 stock split' with no other accounting instruction.
rules.logic: EPS denominator restated retrospectively for all periods presented.

rules.rule: Property dividend at FV at declaration; gain/loss for FV minus book value.
rules.context: Non-cash dividend with no valuation rule stated.
rules.logic: Recognize remeasurement gain/loss in NI; reduce RE by FV.

rules.rule: Liquidating dividend reduces APIC first, then other contributed capital.
rules.logic: Does not touch RE further once RE exhausted.

rules.rule: Dividend payable recognized at declaration date.
rules.logic: Record date is informational; payment date settles cash. Drives cutoff questions.

rules.rule: EPS denominator uses weighted-average shares time-weighted by months outstanding.
Stock splits and stock dividends are retroactive to the earliest period presented.
rules.logic: Restate comparative EPS as if split happened at beginning of earliest period.

rules.rule: Cumulative preferred dividend subtracted from numerator whether declared or not.
rules.context: Cumulative preferred is explicitly named (cumulative is NOT silent default — see A1).

rules.rule: Noncumulative preferred dividend subtracted from numerator only if declared.
rules.context: Silent default per A1; or noncumulative explicitly stated.

rules.rule: Convertible debt uses if-converted method for diluted EPS.
rules.context: Diluted EPS with convertible debt outstanding.
rules.logic: Add interest (net of tax) to numerator; add converted shares to denominator. Test for dilution.

rules.rule: Options/warrants use treasury stock method; include only if avg market > exercise.
Antidilutive securities are excluded; test each individually.
rules.context: Potential dilutive security with antidilutive effect.

rules.rule: Simple capital structure → basic EPS only; complex → basic and diluted.
Lease term = noncancelable + options reasonably certain to exercise + termination penalties reasonably certain to incur.
Lessee classification defaults to operating unless any one of five finance-lease criteria is met.
rules.logic: Ownership transfer / BPO / term ≥75% useful life / PV ≥90% FV / asset specialized to lessee.

rules.rule: Short-term lease (≤12 months, no BPO) may elect straight-line expense.
Initial direct costs are capitalized into ROU.
rules.logic: ROU exceeds lease liability by IDC.

rules.rule: Variable lease payments enter the liability only if based on an index/rate at commencement; truly variable → expense as incurred.
rules.logic: Truly variable payments (e.g., % of sales) never enter the liability.

rules.rule: Lease incentives received reduce ROU.
rules.logic: Reduce ROU asset, not lease liability.

rules.rule: Bond amortization uses effective interest method.
rules.context: Bond discount/premium amortization without method specified.
rules.logic: Straight-line allowed only if not materially different.

rules.rule: Bond interest payments are semi-annual.
rules.context: '10%, 10-year bond' with no payment frequency stated.
rules.logic: Use semi-annual rate and double the periods in PV computations.

rules.rule: Bond issued between interest dates: buyer pays accrued interest at issue, receives it back at next interest date.
rules.logic: Carrying amount excludes the accrued portion.

rules.rule: Notes with no stated rate or an unreasonably low rate are imputed at the market rate.
rules.logic: Exception: short-term trade receivables/payables (≤12 months) and customary trade transactions.

rules.rule: Bond with detachable warrants: allocate proceeds by relative fair value.
rules.logic: Bond CA = proceeds × (bond FV ÷ combined FV).

rules.rule: Bond with nondetachable conversion feature: all proceeds to bond (post-ASU 2020-06).
rules.logic: No separate equity component under US GAAP.

rules.rule: Early extinguishment of debt: gain/loss in NI, continuing operations.
Inventory cost flow method must be stated — no default.
rules.logic: If two methods are mentioned, question tests which applies. Author must state explicitly.

rules.rule: Inventory system (periodic vs perpetual) must be stated — no default.
rules.logic: Periodic counts at period end; perpetual updates every transaction. LIFO can differ.

rules.rule: Apply LCNRV for FIFO and weighted-average inventory.
rules.logic: NRV = selling price − costs to complete and sell.

rules.rule: Apply LCM only for LIFO and retail inventory method.
rules.logic: Market = middle of (ceiling NRV, floor NRV − normal profit, replacement cost).

rules.rule: FOB shipping point: title transfers at seller's dock; in-transit is buyer's.
rules.context: Year-end cutoff with FOB shipping point.
rules.logic: Buyer owns goods on truck at year-end.

rules.rule: FOB destination: title transfers at buyer's dock; in-transit is seller's.
rules.logic: Seller still owns goods en route.

rules.rule: Consignment inventory stays on consignor's books until sold by consignee.
rules.logic: Drives whose inventory at year-end and when revenue is recognized.

rules.rule: Sale with right of return: revenue net of expected returns; refund liability + return asset.
Inventory write-down reversal: US GAAP prohibits; IFRS permits up to original cost.
rules.logic: Switch point between US GAAP and IFRS.

rules.rule: Equity investment ≤20% with no significant influence: FVTNI.
Equity investment 20–50%: presumed significant influence → equity method.
rules.context: Investment in voting common in this range.
rules.logic: Rebuttable presumption but is the default. Investee dividends reduce investment account, not income.

rules.rule: Equity investment >50%: control → consolidation.
Debt security classification must be stated — no default.
rules.context: Debt security held with no classification given.
rules.logic: Trading → FVTNI. AFS → FV through OCI. HTM → amortized cost.

rules.rule: All equity securities outside equity-method/consolidation: FVTNI.
Crypto assets: FVTNI, presented separately on balance sheet (post-ASU 2023-08).
rules.logic: Periodic remeasurement to NI; separate B/S line.

rules.rule: Stock split received by investor: no entry; allocate cost over new total shares.
rules.context: Investor receives a split from investee.
rules.logic: Per-share basis recalculated; total cost unchanged.

rules.rule: Equity-method dividend received: reduce investment, not income.
rules.logic: Income is recognized as investee earns.

rules.rule: Deferred tax balances measured at the enacted future tax rate.
Record a valuation allowance when more likely than not (>50%) the DTA won't realize.
Permanent differences create no DTA/DTL; temporary differences do.
rules.logic: Municipal interest, life insurance proceeds, fines, 50% meals add-back are permanent.

rules.rule: Post-TCJA NOL: indefinite carryforward, no carryback, 80% limit on future usage.
Uncertain tax positions: recognize if MLTN to be sustained; measure at largest amount >50% likely.
Intraperiod tax allocation is required.
rules.context: Continuing ops, discontinued, OCI, direct-to-equity in same period.
rules.logic: Allocate tax effects to each component.

rules.rule: Contract requires approval, identifiable rights, identifiable payment terms, commercial substance, probable collection.
rules.logic: Any failure → no contract → no revenue.

rules.rule: Performance obligation is separate if distinct (capable + separately identifiable in context).
Variable consideration estimated by expected value or most likely amount, constrained to highly probable not to reverse.
Significant financing component recognized if payment ≥1 year from delivery; ignore if <1 year.
rules.logic: Practical expedient for short timing differences.

rules.rule: Allocate transaction price by relative standalone selling price.
Recognition is point-in-time by default; over-time only if one of three criteria met.
rules.context: Goods sale with no over-time language.
rules.logic: Three over-time criteria: simultaneous receive/consume; customer-controlled asset; no alternative use + enforceable right to payment.

rules.rule: Bill-and-hold revenue requires substantive reason, identification, ready to transfer, seller cannot use.
rules.context: Seller holding goods at customer request.

rules.rule: Consignment: no revenue at consignment; recognize when consignee sells.
rules.logic: Consignor still owns and has no revenue.

rules.rule: Principal (controls before transfer) records gross; agent (arranges) records net.
Right of return: revenue net of expected returns; refund liability + return asset.
Sales taxes collected are excluded from transaction price (net).
rules.logic: Sales tax never goes to revenue.

rules.rule: Loss contingency probable + estimable: accrue and disclose.
Loss contingency probable + range, no point estimate better: accrue low end (US GAAP); midpoint (IFRS).
Loss contingency reasonably possible: disclose only.
rules.context: Below probable but more than remote.

rules.rule: Loss contingency remote: ignore (except guarantees, which disclose).
Gain contingency: never accrue; disclose if probable.
Assurance-type warranty always accrued at sale; service-type is a separate PO.
rules.logic: Assurance accrued at sale; service deferred and recognized over service period.

rules.rule: Depreciation method must be stated — no default.
Salvage / residual value defaults to zero if not stated.
Useful life must be stated — no default.
rules.logic: Asset class conventions exist but stem provides the number.

rules.rule: Capitalize costs to acquire and prepare for intended use; expense routine maintenance.
rules.logic: Betterments that extend life are capitalized.

rules.rule: Capitalize interest on self-constructed assets only, during construction, on avg accumulated expenditures, at weighted-avg rate (or specific borrowing).
rules.logic: Capped at actual interest cost incurred.

rules.rule: Long-lived asset impairment (US GAAP, held-and-used): two-step (carrying > undiscounted CF → write down to FV).
Impairment (IFRS): one-step (carrying > recoverable amount = greater of FVLCS or VIU).
Impairment reversal: US GAAP no; IFRS yes (except goodwill).
rules.logic: IFRS reversal limited to original carrying amount net of normal depreciation.

rules.rule: Goodwill impairment (post-ASU 2017-04): one-step (reporting unit FV < carrying → write down by difference, not below zero).
Goodwill amortization: public no, test annually; private may elect 10-year amortization.
R&D (US GAAP): expense as incurred.
rules.logic: Exceptions: software past technological feasibility, materials/equipment with alternative future use (capitalize and depreciate).

rules.rule: R&D (IFRS): research expense; development capitalize if 6 criteria met.
Internal-use software: expense preliminary stage; capitalize application development; expense post-implementation.
rules.context: Internally developed software for own use.

rules.rule: Software for sale: expense to technological feasibility; capitalize through release; amortize greater of revenue ratio or straight-line.
Business combinations use the acquisition method.
Goodwill = consideration + FV of NCI + FV of previously held interest − FV of net identifiable assets.
NCI measured at fair value (US GAAP).
rules.logic: IFRS allows election: FV or proportionate share of net assets.

rules.rule: Intercompany transactions are 100% eliminated.
Intercompany inventory profit: eliminate unrealized portion (in ending inventory not yet sold downstream).
rules.logic: Eliminated in proportion to unsold portion.

rules.rule: Intercompany fixed-asset gain: eliminate gain; adjust depreciation over remaining life.
Acquisition-related costs: expense as incurred.
Contingent consideration at FV at acquisition; equity not remeasured; liability/asset remeasured through earnings.
Measurement period of up to 12 months to finalize provisional amounts.
SOCF method: indirect is most common; direct allowed with indirect reconciliation as supplemental.
rules.logic: Indirect is the standard exam default.

rules.rule: Interest paid: operating (US GAAP).
Interest received: operating (US GAAP).
Dividends received: operating (US GAAP).
Dividends paid: financing (US GAAP).
Income taxes paid: always operating.
Acquisition/sale of PP&E: investing.
Issuance/repurchase of own stock: financing.
Issuance/repayment of debt: financing.
rules.logic: Interest goes operating; principal goes financing.

rules.rule: Non-cash transactions disclosed separately, not in main SOCF.
rules.logic: Schedule of non-cash investing and financing.

rules.rule: NFP net asset classes: without donor restrictions / with donor restrictions (post-ASU 2016-14).
Unconditional pledges: recognize contribution revenue when promise is made, net of estimated uncollectibles.
rules.logic: Revenue at promise, not at cash receipt.

rules.rule: Conditional pledges: no revenue until conditions substantially met.
rules.context: Pledge contingent on matching grant, etc.

rules.rule: Multi-year pledge: present-value at pledge date; subsequent accretion is contribution revenue, not interest income.
rules.logic: Discount unwind classified differently than for-profit interest.

rules.rule: Donated services recognized only if (a) create/enhance nonfinancial asset, or (b) require specialized skills that would have been purchased.
Donated materials recognized at fair value at donation date.
Collections (art, artifacts) may elect to capitalize or not if exhibition/preserved/proceeds-of-sale policy.
Endowment investment return: with donor restrictions unless donor specifies otherwise (UPMIFA default).
Functional expense classification (program services + supporting activities) required for all NFPs.
Governmental funds: current financial resources measurement focus.
rules.context: GF, SRF, CPF, DSF, PF reporting.

rules.rule: Proprietary funds & government-wide: economic resources measurement focus.
Governmental funds: modified accrual basis.
rules.logic: Revenues when measurable + available; expenditures when liability incurred.

rules.rule: Proprietary & government-wide: full accrual basis.
rules.logic: Revenue when earned; expense when incurred.

rules.rule: 'Available' = collectible within period or soon enough after period-end (60 days for property taxes).
rules.logic: Property tax availability is the classic exam fact.

rules.rule: Capital assets in governmental funds: not reported as assets; acquisition is an expenditure.
Long-term debt in governmental funds: not reported as a liability; issuance is 'other financing source'.
Encumbrances recorded when commitments are made; reverse at year-end if intent is to honor next year.
Fund balance categories: Nonspendable / Restricted / Committed / Assigned / Unassigned (NRCAU).
GASB hierarchy: GASB → GASB ITGs/Implementation Guides → AICPA literature cleared by GASB → other accounting literature.
IFRS prohibits LIFO; US GAAP permits.
IFRS permits inventory write-down reversal up to original cost; US GAAP prohibits.
IFRS PP&E: cost or revaluation model; US GAAP: cost only.
rules.context: Subsequent PP&E measurement under IFRS marker.

rules.rule: IFRS impairment: one-step recoverable amount; US GAAP: two-step undiscounted CF first.
IFRS permits impairment reversal (except goodwill); US GAAP prohibits.
IFRS capitalizes development costs if 6 criteria met; US GAAP expenses R&D.
IFRS interest on SOCF: operating or financing; US GAAP: operating.
IFRS dividends paid: operating or financing; US GAAP: financing.
IFRS loss-contingency range: midpoint; US GAAP: low end if no amount better.
IFRS convertible debt: split between liability and equity; US GAAP: single instrument (post-2020-06).
IFRS bank overdrafts: cash and cash equivalents if integral to cash management; US GAAP: financing.
Extraordinary items eliminated under US GAAP (since 2015); prohibited under IFRS.
Fair value = exit price in an orderly transaction between market participants at the measurement date.
Use the principal market (greatest volume and activity) even if a better price exists elsewhere.
Most advantageous market is used only if there is no principal market.
Nonfinancial assets measured at highest and best use from market-participant perspective.
FV hierarchy: L1 > L2 > L3; disclose at the lowest level of significant input.
rules.logic: Lowest-level significant input drives the disclosed level.

rules.rule: Transaction costs are not included in fair value.
rules.logic: They affect choice of most advantageous market, not FV itself.

rules.rule: Reporting period defaults to calendar year, annual, comparative with prior year.
Functional currency defaults to US dollars.
Going concern assumed; disclosure required only if substantial doubt exists.
rules.logic: Substantial doubt = within one year of issuance.

rules.rule: Items are material unless stem says 'immaterial'.
Cash equivalents = investments ≤3 months to maturity when purchased.
rules.logic: 6-month-original-maturity T-bill is not a cash equivalent even if now <3 months.

rules.rule: Accrual basis assumed for GAAP entities.
rules.logic: Cash basis is a special-purpose framework.

rules.rule: Allowance for doubtful accounts method must be stated — no default.
rules.logic: % of sales (I/S approach) vs aging-of-receivables (B/S approach).

rules.rule: Specific identification used only for unique, large-dollar items.
Bond covenant violation: reclassify debt as current unless lender has waived right to call.
Subsequent events Type 1 (recognized): conditions existed at B/S date → adjust the financial statements.
rules.context: Lawsuit settled before issuance, condition existed at B/S date.

rules.rule: Subsequent events Type 2 (non-recognized): conditions arose after B/S date → disclose only.
Pension expense: service cost in operating; other components below operating income.
rules.logic: Interest, expected return, amortization of PSC/gains-losses are non-operating.

rules.rule: Pension funded status = PBO − plan assets at FV; single net asset or liability on B/S.
Discontinued operations = disposal of a component representing a strategic shift with major effect.
rules.logic: A single fixed asset is not enough.

rules.rule: 'Component of an entity' = operating segment, reportable segment, reporting unit, subsidiary, or asset group whose ops and CFs are clearly distinguishable.
OCI components = PUFI: Pension adjustments, Unrealized AFS-debt gains/losses, Foreign currency translation, Interest-rate (cash-flow) hedges.
FX translation: functional = local → current rate method (OCI/CTA); functional = USD → remeasurement (gain/loss to NI).
rules.logic: Method choice determines where FX P/L lands.

rules.rule: Statement of changes in equity is required as a basic statement.
10-K = annual audited; 10-Q = quarterly reviewed; 8-K = current material events.
Large accelerated filer deadlines: 10-K 60 days; 10-Q 40 days; 8-K 4 business days.
First-time IFRS adopter applies IFRS retrospectively as if always used; restate comparative period.
Interim reporting (US GAAP): each interim period is an integral part of the annual period; discrete view for certain items (taxes use annual effective rate).
rules.logic: Integral view default with discrete exceptions.

rules.rule: Segment reporting threshold: revenue ≥10% of total, OR profit/loss ≥10% of greater of combined profits or combined losses, OR assets ≥10% of total; plus the 75% reportable-segments aggregate rule.
rules.logic: Three quantitative thresholds plus the aggregate-coverage rule.
